title: ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. capillaceus
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. capillaceus, also known as the creeping buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. capillaceus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health issues.
